---
title: "Mesfin Genie"
---

::: {.hero-wrap}
::: {.hero}
::: {}
<div class="eyebrow">Health Economics · Choice Modelling · Behavioural Science</div>

# Mesfin Genie, PhD

## Senior Lecturer in Health Economics  
## Newcastle Business School · The University of Newcastle

I study how patients, citizens and decision makers make trade-offs in complex health and policy settings. My work combines health economics, discrete choice experiments, behavioural economics, eye tracking, vaccine policy, health technology assessment, and applied policy evaluation.

::: {.hero-actions}
[Publications](publications.qmd){.btn-site .btn-primary}
[Research](research.qmd){.btn-site .btn-secondary}
[Projects](projects.qmd){.btn-site .btn-secondary}
[CV](cv.qmd){.btn-site .btn-secondary}
:::
:::

::: {.profile-panel}
<div class="portrait">MG</div>
<div class="panel-caption"><strong>Research focus.</strong> Health preference research, DCEs, information processing, vaccine policy, HTA, clinical outcome assessment, and decision support tools.</div>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.metrics}
::: {.metric}
<div class="num">41</div>
<div class="label">Journal articles listed by UoN</div>
:::
::: {.metric}
<div class="num">8</div>
<div class="label">Grants listed by UoN</div>
:::
::: {.metric}
<div class="num">$1.24m</div>
<div class="label">Funding listed by UoN</div>
:::
::: {.metric}
<div class="num">9</div>
<div class="label">Current/past supervisions</div>
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.section-title}
## Research at a glance

Selected areas that define my current programme of work.
:::

::: {.grid-3}
::: {.card}
### Health economics and policy
Economic evaluation, health-system reform, health technology assessment, vaccine policy, public preferences, and policy acceptability.
:::
::: {.card}
### Choice modelling
Discrete choice experiments, mixed logit, latent class models, heterogeneity, equity–efficiency trade-offs, WTP, and stated preference methods.
:::
::: {.card}
### Behavioural health economics
Attention, information processing, numeracy, feedback, eye tracking, decision quality, and choice architecture in health preference tasks.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.section-title}
## Selected recent work

Recent publications organised around choice modelling, vaccine policy, health preference methods, and applied health economics.
:::

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
### Health preference methods
<div class="pub"><div class="pub-title">Guidance or Misdirection? Unpacking the Role of Feedback in Health Preference Assessments</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie, Reed and Ozdemir · <em>Health Economics</em> · 2026</div></div>
<div class="pub"><div class="pub-title">Fit for purpose? Assessing the robustness of discrete choice experiment designs in the context of evolving health technologies</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie, Watson, Schwarzinger and Luchini · <em>Social Science & Medicine</em> · 2026</div></div>
:::
::: {.card}
### Vaccine policy and public preferences
<div class="pub"><div class="pub-title">No Jab, No Access? Preferences for Vaccine Mandates in Europe and Australia</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie et al. · <em>Australian and New Zealand Journal of Public Health</em> · 2026</div></div>
<div class="pub"><div class="pub-title">An open repository of COVID-19 vaccine mandate studies with a worked scoping review of quasi-experimental evidence</div><div class="pub-meta">Martinenghi et al. · <em>npj Vaccines</em> · 2026</div></div>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.section-title}
## Current priorities

The website is structured so these streams can expand into project pages, outputs, software, and policy-facing materials.
:::

::: {.grid-4}
:::

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
### MandEval and vaccine mandates
Mandate design, public acceptability, quasi-experimental evidence, international preference heterogeneity, and decision tools for future pandemic policy.
:::
::: {.card}
### Attention and choice
Eye tracking, attribute ordering, information acquisition, early attention, and how presentation affects preference estimates and decision quality.
:::
::: {.card}
### Decision support tools
Online tools translating preference evidence and economic evaluation outputs into transparent policy, clinical, and agricultural decision support.
:::
::: {.card}
### Equity and value assessment
Incorporating concern for worse-off groups, distributional preferences, and fairness into preference-based value assessment.
:::
:::
:::
